parent.text: TPU 8t, optimized for training and TPU 8i, optimized for inference. Looking good!
parent.name: Sundar Pichai
parent.embed_html:
TPU 8t, optimized for training and TPU 8i, optimized for inference.

Looking good! pic.twitter.com/pTrblfF8mU

— Sundar Pichai (@sundarpichai) April 22, 2026

ai_summary: Sundar Pichai announced Google's new TPU 8t (optimized for training) and TPU 8i (optimized for inference) chips, representing advances in AI compute infrastructure. Elon Musk's reply acknowledging that TPUs are underrated highlights the critical importance of specialized hardware acceleration for AI/ML workloads—a core competitive factor in the AI infrastructure race that directly impacts training efficiency and inference costs for companies building large language models and AI systems.
embed_html:
TPUs are underrated

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 22, 2026

text:
@aaronburnett 100TW of compute per year would be 200 times the US economy EVERY YEAR. 

Dollars won’t even be used if we get to that point.
summary: @aaronburnett 100TW of compute per year would be 200 times the US economy EVERY YEAR. Dollars won’t even be used if we get to that point.

parent.text: Paying 6.6T for 100 Terawatts of Compute a year is... 1) an insane operational goal 2) almost 3 orders of magnitude less per GW than we pay for 1 GW of compute today. Humans let alone investors cannot fathom this.
parent.name: Aaron Burnett
parent.embed_html:
Paying 6.6T for 100 Terawatts of Compute a year is...

1) an insane operational goal
2) almost 3 orders of magnitude less per GW than we pay for 1 GW of compute today.

Humans let alone investors cannot fathom this. https://t.co/J8xHh2OYsp

— Aaron Burnett (@aaronburnett) April 21, 2026

ai_summary: Aaron Burnett highlights an ambitious compute infrastructure goal of 100 Terawatts annually for $6.6 trillion—representing a dramatic 1000x improvement in cost-efficiency compared to current compute pricing—while Elon contextualizes the scale by noting this would represent 200x US annual GDP, suggesting such transformative compute abundance could fundamentally reshape economics and currency systems. This relates to xAI's infrastructure scaling ambitions and the broader AI/ML compute demands required to train and run next-generation AI models at massive scale.
embed_html:
100TW of compute per year would be 200 times the US economy EVERY YEAR.

Dollars won’t even be used if we get to that point.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 22, 2026
